MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-19-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER TO 1ST, 2ND, AND 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN VIEW OF COURT'S RULING ON SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 4TH (EXTORTION AND THREATS) CAUSE OF ACTION WHICH IS A CRIME AND NOT A RECOGNIZED CIVIL CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE THE DATES OF THE ACTIVITIES THAT HE ALLEGES CAUSED HIM DAMAGE IN EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS IN SUPPORT (WHEN THE STATEMENTS WERE MADE, WHAT WAS SAID, AND TO WHOM.) AS TO 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WHETHER HE IS CLAIMING INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS OR PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC RELATIONS, AND FACTS, INCLUDING DATES OF ALLEGED INTERFERENCES. AS TO 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING NEGLIGENCE AND SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING NEGLIGENCE OR SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. ORDER ON DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (SLAPP) THE FIRST, SECOND, THIRD AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION OF COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES (CCP 425.16); REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS.
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-19-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 1ST (FRAUD), 2ND (HARASSMENT), AND 6TH (INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS) CAUSES OF ACTION. THE GRAVAMEN OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION IS PROTECTED ACTIVITY: COMPLAINTS TO VARIOUS OFFICIALS, CITY DEPARTMENTS, AND GOVERNMENTAL AGENCIES REGARDING SAFETY, FIRE, AND BUILDING ISSUES. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO MEET HIS BURDEN TO SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. THESE ACTIVITIES ARISE FROM JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS AND ARE PROTECTED UNDER THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE OF CC SECTION 47(B). MOTION DENIED AS TO 3RD (DEFAMATION) CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO SHOW ACTIVITIES WERE PROTECTED UNDER THE FIRST PRONG. DEFENDANT MAY MAKE A SEPARATE MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ELIABETH LEE'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT AND EACH AND EVERY CAUSE OF ACTION.

APPEARANCES: Martin Eng, 246-1111 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Thomas S. Gelini, Esq. of Bennett, Samuelsen, Reynolds & Allard 510/444-7688 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - AS TO THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING NEGLIGENCE OR SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN GIVEN TWO OPPORTUNITIES TO CORRECT THE SAME DEFICIENCIES IN HIS COMPLAINT AND HAS FAILED TO DO SO. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-24-2010 Continued To Feb-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT ELIABETH LEE'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S AMENDED COMPLAINT AND EACH AND EVERY CAUSE OF ACTION.

APPEARANCES: Thomas S. Gelini, Esq. of Bennett, Samuelsen, Reynolds & Allard 510/444-7688 for deft & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH ONE FINAL 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO BRING THESE CLAIMS. NONE OF THE ALLEGED CAUSES OF ACTION REQUIRE OWNERSHIP OF THE PROPERTY AS A PREREQUISITE TO BRINGING SUIT. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MAY NOT ALLEGE STATEMENTS THAT TOOK PLACE BEFORE 8-11-09. THESE ARE RES JUDICATA OF CASE #478790. SEE ALPHA MECHANICAL V. TRAVELERS CASUALTY (2005) 133 CAL.APP.4TH 1319, 1330-31. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE STATEMENTS THAT CLEARLY OCCURRED AFTER 8-11-09. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD WHETHER HE IS CLAIMING INTERFERENCE WITH CONTRACTUAL RELATIONS OR PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC RELATIONS AND FACTS, INCLUDING DATES, OF ALLEGED INTERFERENCES. AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SHOWING NEGLIGENCE AND SERIOUS EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn To Dismiss Pltf'S Complaint Following Pltf'S Failure To Amend Within Time Allowed By The Court
Off Calendar Sep-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher B. Allard of Bennett Samuelsen Reynolds & Allard (510.444.7688) for defendant and moving party; Martin Eng (no card provided) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona

Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT ELIZABETH LEE'S MOTION TO DISMISS PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT FOLLOWING PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO AMEND WITHIN TIME ALLOWED BY THE COURT is denied. Mr. Eng was directed to serve his first amended complaint on defendant by mail today. Defendant's request for 30 days to answer was granted. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2010 Continued To Oct-08-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ELIZABETH LEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Thomas S. Gelini of Bennett Samuelsen Reynolds & Allard (510.444.7688) for defendant and moving party; Martin Eng (246.1111) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona. Mr. Eng did not comply with Local Rule 8.3(D). The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer to 1st, 2nd, and 6th causes of action off calendar as moot in view of Court's ruling on special motion to strike. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 4th (extortion and threats) cause of action which is a crime and not a recognized civil cause of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to remaining causes of action. Plaintiff must allege the dates of the activities that he alleges caused him damage in each cause of action. As to the 3rd cause of action, plaintiff must plead factual allegations in support (when the statements were made, what was said, and to whom.) As to 5th cause of action, plaintiff must plead whether he is claiming interference with contractual relations or prospective economic relations, and facts, including dates of alleged interferences. As to 7th cause of action, plaintiff must plead facts showing negligence and serious emotional distress. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ELIZABETH LEE'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Appearances: Thomas S. Gelini of Bennett Samuelsen Reynolds & Allard (510.444.7688) for defendant and moving party; Martin Eng (246.1111) plaintiff and responding party appearing in propria persona. Mr. Eng did not comply with Local Rule 8.3(D). The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted without leave to amend as to 1st (fraud), 2nd (harassment), and 6th (intentional infliction of emotional distress) causes of action. The gravamen of these causes of action is protected activity: complaints to various officials, city departments, and governmental agencies regarding safety, fire, and building issues. Plaintiff fails to meet his burden to show a likelihood of success on the merits. These activities arise from judicial proceedings and are protected under the litigation privilege of CC section 47(b). Motion denied as to 3rd (defamation) cause of action for failure to show activities were protected under the first prong. Defendant may make a separate motion for attorney fees and costs. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
